claim: A 36-attempt approved GLM-5.2 micro-pilot was accounted for, with 33 persisted sanitized output rows and 3 prior-attempt accounting rows.
05AK generated descriptive parser-validity, stage, condition, cascade-sequence, and usage/cost metrics from sanitized committed outputs only.
05AK made no API/model calls, did not read the API key, and wrote no raw prompt, raw response, or JSONL artifact.
This does not prove broad GLM-5.2 reliability, provider superiority, or deployment safety.
This does not yet establish Q1-level evidence; it is a small real-LLM micro-pilot requiring aggregation, robustness checks, and cross-model/cross-domain analysis.
Parser validity is not equivalent to substantive decision correctness.
05AL should produce figures/tables and a stronger reliability-cascade interpretation, still without new API calls unless explicitly approved.
